semantic_math.output:
Let $V$ be the volume of the bathtub.
Rate of Faucet A ($R_A$):
$$R_A = \frac{V}{12} \text{ per minute}$$

Rate of Faucet B ($R_B$):
$$R_B = \frac{V}{18} \text{ per minute}$$

Rate of Drain ($R_D$):
$$R_D = \frac{V}{36} \text{ per minute}$$

semantic_math.output:
The formula for compound interest is:
$$A = P \left(1 + \frac{r}{n}\right)^{nt}$$
Where:
$P$ = Principal amount = \$5,000
$r$ = Annual interest rate (as a decimal) = 0.045
$n$ = Number of times interest is compounded per year = 12 (monthly)
